_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Application of traction – Crutchfield tong cervical spine traction
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years of experience

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)- confirming the diagnosis/MRI/CT

ppd_preauth.q: Were the clinical notes and x-ray report submitted are indicative of procedure?
Were clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)- /MRI/CT confirming the diagnosis submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Were the clinical notes and x-ray report submitted are indicative of procedure being done?
Was the invoice/barcode of implant submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or diagnostic imaging (X-ray/MRI/CT) not indicative of Crutchfield tong cervical spine traction procedure.
Mandatory clinical notes are missing essential details such as history, symptoms, evaluation findings, or planned management.
Diagnostic imaging (X-ray/MRI/CT) is not labelled with patient ID, date, or side, or fails to confirm cervical vertebrae fracture.
Post-procedure imaging study (Xray) not submitted at the time of claim processing.
Detailed procedure/operative note not submitted with the claim.
Invoice or barcode for the implant not submitted at the time of claim.

clinical_key_pointers: Crutchfield tong is attached to the skull to hyperextend the head and neck of the patient with fractured cervical vertebrae and to immobilize and align the vertebrae.
A CT scan or X-ray can be used to confirm the diagnosis.
The tips of the tongs are inserted into small burr holes drilled in each parietal bone of the skull.
The surrounding skin is sutured and covered with dressing.
A rope tied to the center of the tongs passes over a pulley at the head of the bed and is attached to a weight of 10 to 20 pounds, which hangs freely.
The insertion sites of the tongs are inspected and cleaned every 1 to 2 hours; any formed crusts are removed with hydrogen peroxide or normal saline twice a day or as required.
Post traction X-rays are taken regularly plus tightening of crutchfield tong and adjustment of traction is required regularly based on type of reduction achieved on post traction Xrays.
